evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: The engineer ensures data quality through testing and validation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — data quality, testing, validation, CI/CD, quality-focused mindset — with no explicit velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: collaborate with business analysts and clients
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Consultancy/project environment serving client business decisions without explicit financial reporting or regulatory language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Proven production experience with Snowflake and dbt
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named production tools (Snowflake, dbt) and an established Medallion architecture pattern in responsibilities indicate a growing, structured data practice rather than early-stage or fully mature.
evidence.jd_authorship: designing and maintaining scalable data models using the Medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold layers)
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named architecture pattern, tools (Snowflake, dbt), and specific practices (Data Vault, dimensional modeling) make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: collaborate with business analysts and clients
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Consultancy context serving external clients with data-driven insights places this in commercial.
evidence.autonomy_level: requires defining semantic layers to establish consistent business logic and metrics that downstream users rely upon
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role defines semantic layers and business logic (strategic) while also building pipelines and collaborating with clients on specific requests (execution).
evidence.ai_role: leveraging AI tools daily for coding and documentation
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI tools daily to accelerate their own coding and documentation work.
evidence.testing_framing: The engineer ensures data quality through testing and validation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality are framed as an owned responsibility with an action verb (ensures), not just a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: establish consistent business logic and metrics that downstream users rely upon
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: reliability and consistency of downstream metrics is a recurring concern, but framed as delivery/data quality craft rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing and maintaining scalable data models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of designing new semantic layers/pipelines (greenfield) and maintaining existing Medallion-architecture models (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
